title: OP-REPORT: Infiltrating the Windows Outpost
summary: A complete adversary simulation demonstrating initial access, internal reconnaissance, and log evasion techniques on a Windows 10 target.

title: Understanding IP Geolocation: How It Works and Why It Matters
summary: A technical deep-dive into how IP addresses are mapped to physical locations, the differences between IPv4 and IPv6, and practical cybersecurity applications of geolocation data.

title: DNS Records Explained: A Complete Guide to Domain Name System
summary: Comprehensive guide covering A, AAAA, MX, NS, and TXT records, DNS security threats like spoofing and hijacking, and the critical role of SPF, DKIM, and DMARC.

title: Cryptographic Hash Functions: A Security Practitioner's Guide
summary: Comparison of MD5, SHA-1, SHA-256, SHA-384, and SHA-512 algorithms with real-world applications in file integrity, digital forensics, password storage, and malware detection.

title: Base64 Encoding Explained: What It Is, How It Works, and When to Use It
summary: Technical overview of Base64 encoding mechanics, common use cases in email, APIs, and JWT tokens, and why Base64 is NOT encryption.

title: Understanding Firewalls: Types, Functions, and Best Practices
summary: Complete guide to firewall types including packet filtering, stateful inspection, WAF, and NGFW with configuration best practices and common misconfigurations.

title: Social Engineering Attacks: How Hackers Exploit Human Psychology
summary: Deep dive into phishing, pretexting, baiting, tailgating, vishing, and smishing techniques with psychological principles behind each attack and defense strategies.

title: SSL/TLS Explained: How HTTPS Keeps Your Data Secure
summary: Technical breakdown of the TLS handshake, certificate types (DV, OV, EV), common vulnerabilities, and best practices for modern encryption.

title: Password Security in 2026: Best Practices and Common Mistakes
summary: How brute force, dictionary, credential stuffing, and rainbow table attacks work, with guidance on passphrases, password managers, and secure storage using bcrypt and Argon2.

title: DDoS Attacks Explained: How They Work and How to Defend Against Them
summary: Overview of volumetric, protocol, and application layer DDoS attacks with defense strategies including rate limiting, CDN protection, and anycast routing.

title: How VPNs Work: A Technical Guide to Virtual Private Networks
summary: Technical explanation of VPN tunneling, protocol comparison (WireGuard, OpenVPN, IPSec/IKEv2), and honest analysis of what VPNs do and don't protect against.

title: Malware Types Explained: Viruses, Trojans, Ransomware, and More
summary: Comprehensive guide to viruses, worms, trojans, ransomware, spyware, and rootkits with notable attack examples and modern detection and prevention strategies.

title: Two-Factor Authentication (2FA): Why It Matters and How It Works
summary: Comparison of SMS, TOTP, hardware keys (FIDO2), push notifications, and biometrics with implementation best practices and attack statistics.

title: Incident Response: A Step-by-Step Guide for Security Teams
summary: NIST-based incident response framework covering preparation, detection and analysis, containment and eradication, and post-incident lessons learned.

title: Network Reconnaissance: Techniques Used by Attackers and Defenders
summary: Guide to passive and active reconnaissance including OSINT, DNS enumeration, port scanning with Nmap, vulnerability scanning, and defensive countermeasures.
